session.assistant: The test files use the CLI flag strings directly. Let me check what patterns are actually there:
session.tool_result:
253: """Test that --force reuses an unassigned slot with checkout_branch, not add_worktree. 255: Regression test for issue #4173: When pool is full and --force is used, 309: # Create a new branch with --force (should reuse the slot) 311: cli, ["br", "create", "--force", "new-branch"], obj=test_ctx, catch_exceptions=False 343:def test_branch_create_for_plan_creates_branch_and_impl_folder(tmp_path) -> None: 344: """Test that --for-plan creates branch, slot, and .impl/ folder.""" 395: cli, ["br", "create", "--for-plan", "123"], obj=test_ctx, catch_exceptions=False 421:def test_branch_create_for_plan_with_issue_url(tmp_path) -> None: 422: """Test that --for-plan accepts GitHub issue URLs.""" 472: ["br", "create", "--for-plan", "https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/456"], 482:def test_branch_create_for_plan_with_no_slot_skips_impl() -> None: 483: """Test that --for-plan with --no-slot creates branch but not .impl folder.""" 534: ["br", "create", "--for-plan", "100", "--no-slot"], 546:def test_branch_create_fails_with_both_branch_and_for_plan() -> None: 547: """Test that specifying both BRANCH and --for-plan fails.""" 571: ["br", "create", "my-branch", "--for-plan", "123"], 577: assert "Cannot specify both BRANCH and --for-plan" in result.output 580:def test_branch_create_fails_without_branch_or_for_plan() -> None: 581: """Test that omitting both BRANCH and --for-plan fails.""" 606: assert "Must provide BRANCH argument or --for-plan option" in result.output 653:def test_branch_create_for_plan_stacks_on_current_branch() -> None: 654: """Test that --for-plan stacks on current branch when not on trunk.""" 706: cli, ["br", "create", "--for-plan", "200"], obj=test_ctx, catch_exceptions=False 853:def test_branch_create_for_plan_stacks_in_place_creates_impl() -> None: 854: """Test that --for-plan from assigned slot stacks in place AND creates .impl/.""" 919: cli, ["br", "create", "--for-plan", "300"], obj=test_ctx, catch_exceptions=False
[Showing results with pagination = limit: 30, offset: 0]

260:# --- --for-plan tests --- 263:def test_checkout_for_plan_creates_impl_folder() -> None: 264: """Test that --for-plan resolves plan, creates branch, and sets up .impl/. 298: result = runner.invoke(branch_group, ["checkout", "--for-plan", "500"], obj=ctx) 315:def test_checkout_for_plan_prints_activation_when_sync_status_fails() -> None: 316: """Test that --for-plan checkout prints activation instructions even when sync status fails. 319: instructions from being printed after --for-plan checkout. This happens when 370: result = runner.invoke(branch_group, ["checkout", "--for-plan", "600"], obj=ctx) 376:def test_checkout_for_plan_error_both_branch_and_for_plan() -> None: 377: """Test that providing both BRANCH and --for-plan fails.""" 390: branch_group, ["checkout", "my-branch", "--for-plan", "123"], obj=ctx 394: assert "Cannot specify both BRANCH and --for-plan" in result.output 397:def test_checkout_for_plan_error_neither_branch_nor_for_plan() -> None: 398: """Test that providing neither BRANCH nor --for-plan fails.""" 413: assert "Must provide BRANCH argument or --for-plan option" in result.output 416:def test_checkout_for_plan_planned_pr_stacks_on_base_ref() -> None: 417: """--for-plan with planned_pr backend tracks with base_ref_name from plan metadata.""" 452: branch_group, ["checkout", "--for-plan", "600", "--script"], obj=ctx 517:def test_checkout_for_plan_planned_pr_falls_back_to_trunk_without_base_ref() -> None: 553: branch_group, ["checkout", "--for-plan", "601", "--script"], obj=ctx 565:def test_checkout_for_plan_rebases_onto_stale_parent() -> None: 566: """--for-plan with stacked parent rebases onto parent before gt track. 606: branch_group, ["checkout", "--for-plan", "602", "--script"], obj=ctx 623:def test_checkout_for_plan_skips_rebase_for_trunk_parent() -> None: 624: """--for-plan with trunk parent does not rebase, only tracks. 663: branch_group, ["checkout", "--for-plan", "603", "--script"], obj=ctx 678:def test_checkout_for_plan_updates_stale_local_parent() -> None: 679: """--for-plan syncs stale local parent branch with origin before gt track. 723: branch_group, ["checkout", "--for-plan", "604", "--script"], obj=ctx
... [1 lines omitted] ...

    "--for-plan",
    "for_plan",
    type=str,
    default=None,
    help="PR number or URL with erk-pr label",
)","new_string":"@click.option(
    "--for-pr",
    "for_pr",
    type=str,
    default=None,
    help="PR number or URL with erk-pr label",

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"def branch_checkout(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    branch: str | None,
    for_plan: str | None,
    script: bool,
) -> None:","new_string":"def branch_checkout(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    branch: str | None,
    for_pr: str | None,
    script: bool,
) -> None:"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"    """Checkout BRANCH by finding and switching to its worktree.

    Prints the activation path for the target worktree.

    This command finds which worktree has the specified branch checked out
    and switches to it. If the branch exists but isn't checked out anywhere,
    a worktree is automatically created. If the branch exists on origin but
    not locally, a tracking branch and worktree are created automatically.

    Use --for-plan to resolve a PR and set up .erk/impl-context/ after checkout.

    Examples:

        erk br co feature/user-auth      # Checkout existing worktree

        erk br co unchecked-branch       # Auto-create worktree

        erk br co --for-plan 123         # Checkout PR branch with .erk/impl-context/ setup

    If multiple worktrees contain the branch, all options are shown.
    """","new_string":"    """Checkout BRANCH by finding and switching to its worktree.

    Prints the activation path for the target worktree.

    This command finds which worktree has the specified branch checked out
    and switches to it. If the branch exists but isn't checked out anywhere,
    a worktree is automatically created. If the branch exists on origin but
    not locally, a tracking branch and worktree are created automatically.

    Use --for-pr to resolve a PR and set up .erk/impl-context/ after checkout.

    Examples:

        erk br co feature/user-auth      # Checkout existing worktree

        erk br co unchecked-branch       # Auto-create worktree

        erk br co --for-pr 123           # Checkout PR branch with .erk/impl-context/ setup

    If multiple worktrees contain the branch, all options are shown.
    """"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"    handler = script_error_handler(ctx) if script else contextlib.nullcontext()
    with handler:
        _branch_checkout_impl(
            ctx,
            branch=branch,
            for_plan=for_plan,
            script=script,
        )","new_string":"    handler = script_error_handler(ctx) if script else contextlib.nullcontext()
    with handler:
        _branch_checkout_impl(
            ctx,
            branch=branch,
            for_pr=for_pr,
            script=script,
        )"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"def _branch_checkout_impl(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    branch: str | None,
    for_plan: str | None,
    script: bool,
) -> None:","new_string":"def _branch_checkout_impl(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    branch: str | None,
    for_pr: str | None,
    script: bool,
) -> None:"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"    """Implementation body for branch_checkout — separated for error wrapping."""
    # Mutual exclusivity validation
    if for_plan is not None and branch is not None:
        user_output(
            "Error: Cannot specify both BRANCH and --for-plan.\n"
            "Use --for-plan to derive branch name from PR, or provide BRANCH directly."
        )
        raise SystemExit(1) from None

    if for_plan is None and branch is None:
        user_output("Error: Must provide BRANCH argument or --for-plan option.")
        raise SystemExit(1) from None","new_string":"    """Implementation body for branch_checkout — separated for error wrapping."""
    # Mutual exclusivity validation
    if for_pr is not None and branch is not None:
        user_output(
            "Error: Cannot specify both BRANCH and --for-pr.\n"
            "Use --for-pr to derive branch name from PR, or provide BRANCH directly."
        )
        raise SystemExit(1) from None

    if for_pr is None and branch is None:
        user_output("Error: Must provide BRANCH argument or --for-pr option.")
        raise SystemExit(1) from None"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"    # Plan setup - fetches plan and derives branch name if --for-plan is used
    setup: PlanBranchSetup | None = None
    plan: Plan | None = None

    if for_plan is not None:
        pr_number = parse_issue_identifier(for_plan)","new_string":"    # Plan setup - fetches plan and derives branch name if --for-pr is used
    setup: PlanBranchSetup | None = None
    plan: Plan | None = None

    if for_pr is not None:
        pr_number = parse_issue_identifier(for_pr)"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"    # At this point, branch is guaranteed to be set
    assert branch is not None

    # If --for-plan, handle branch creation/tracking before checkout
    if setup is not None:","new_string":"    # At this point, branch is guaranteed to be set
    assert branch is not None

    # If --for-pr, handle branch creation/tracking before checkout
    if setup is not None:"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"            # Root not available or not trunk - checkout in current worktree
            # Ensure branch exists (may need to create tracking branch)
            # Skip if --for-plan already handled branch creation
            if setup is None:","new_string":"            # Root not available or not trunk - checkout in current worktree
            # Ensure branch exists (may need to create tracking branch)
            # Skip if --for-pr already handled branch creation

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"    if len(matching_worktrees) == 1:
        # Exactly one worktree contains this branch
        target_worktree = matching_worktrees[0]

        # Set up impl folder if --for-plan was used
        if setup is not None:","new_string":"    if len(matching_worktrees) == 1:
        # Exactly one worktree contains this branch
        target_worktree = matching_worktrees[0]

        # Set up impl folder if --for-pr was used
        if setup is not None:"}

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/create_cmd.py","old_string":"def branch_create(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    branch_name: str | None,
    for_plan: str | None,
    no_slot: bool,
    new_slot: bool,
    force: bool,
    *,
    create_only: bool,
    dangerous: bool,
    docker: bool,
    codespace: bool,
    codespace_name: str | None,
    script: bool,
) -> None:","new_string":"def branch_create(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    branch_name: str | None,
    for_pr: str | None,
    no_slot: bool,
    new_slot: bool,
    force: bool,
    *,
    create_only: bool,
    dangerous: bool,
    docker: bool,
    codespace: bool,
    codespace_name: str | None,
    script: bool,
) -> None:"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/create_cmd.py","old_string":"    """Create a NEW branch and optionally assign it to a pool slot.

    BRANCH is the name of the new git branch to create.

    By default, the command will:
    1. Verify the branch does NOT already exist (fails if it does)
    2. Create the branch from trunk
    3. Find the next available slot in the pool
    4. Create a worktree for that slot
    5. Assign the branch to the slot

    Use --no-slot to create a branch without assigning it to a slot.
    Use --for-plan to create a branch from a plan with erk-plan label.
    Use `erk br assign` to assign an EXISTING branch to a slot.
    """","new_string":"    """Create a NEW branch and optionally assign it to a pool slot.

    BRANCH is the name of the new git branch to create.

    By default, the command will:
    1. Verify the branch does NOT already exist (fails if it does)
    2. Create the branch from trunk
    3. Find the next available slot in the pool
    4. Create a worktree for that slot
    5. Assign the branch to the slot

    Use --no-slot to create a branch without assigning it to a slot.
    Use --for-pr to create a branch from a plan with erk-plan label.
    Use `erk br assign` to assign an EXISTING branch to a slot.
    """"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/create_cmd.py","old_string":"    # Mutual exclusivity validation
    if for_plan is not None and branch_name is not None:
        user_output(
            "Error: Cannot specify both BRANCH and --for-plan.\n"
            "Use --for-plan to derive branch name from PR, or provide BRANCH directly."
        )
        raise SystemExit(1) from None

    if for_plan is None and branch_name is None:
        user_output("Error: Must provide BRANCH argument or --for-plan option.")
        raise SystemExit(1) from None","new_string":"    # Mutual exclusivity validation
    if for_pr is not None and branch_name is not None:
        user_output(
            "Error: Cannot specify both BRANCH and --for-pr.\n"
            "Use --for-pr to derive branch name from PR, or provide BRANCH directly."
        )
        raise SystemExit(1) from None

    if for_pr is None and branch_name is None:
        user_output("Error: Must provide BRANCH argument or --for-pr option.")
        raise SystemExit(1) from None"}

    if for_plan is not None:
        pr_number = parse_issue_identifier(for_plan)","new_string":"    # Plan setup - fetches plan and derives branch name if --for-pr is used

    if for_pr is not None:
        pr_number = parse_issue_identifier(for_pr)"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/create_cmd.py","old_string":"    # If --no-slot is specified, we're done (but warn about impl context if --for-plan was used)","new_string":"    # If --no-slot is specified, we're done (but warn about impl context if --for-pr was used)"}

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/tui/screens/plan_detail_screen.py","old_string":"    def action_copy_prepare(self) -> None:
        """Copy basic prepare command to clipboard."""
        cmd = f"erk br co --for-plan {self._row.pr_number}"
        self._copy_and_notify(cmd)","new_string":"    def action_copy_prepare(self) -> None:
        """Copy basic prepare command to clipboard."""
        cmd = f"erk br co --for-pr {self._row.pr_number}"
        self._copy_and_notify(cmd)"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/tui/screens/plan_detail_screen.py","old_string":"    def action_copy_prepare_activate(self) -> None:
        """Copy one-liner to prepare worktree and start implementation."""
        cmd = (
            f'source "$(erk br co --for-plan {self._row.pr_number} --script)"'
            " && erk implement --dangerous"
        )
        self._copy_and_notify(cmd)","new_string":"    def action_copy_prepare_activate(self) -> None:
        """Copy one-liner to prepare worktree and start implementation."""
        cmd = (
            f'source "$(erk br co --for-pr {self._row.pr_number} --script)"'
            " && erk implement --dangerous"
        )
        self._copy_and_notify(cmd)"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/tui/screens/plan_detail_screen.py","old_string":"        elif command_id == "copy_prepare":

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/tui/screens/plan_detail_screen.py","old_string":"            # Prepare commands
            prepare_cmd = f"erk br co --for-plan {self._row.pr_number}"
            with Container(classes="command-row"):
                yield Label("[1]", classes="command-key")
                yield CopyableLabel(prepare_cmd, prepare_cmd)","new_string":"            # Prepare commands
            prepare_cmd = f"erk br co --for-pr {self._row.pr_number}"
            with Container(classes="command-row"):
                yield Label("[1]", classes="command-key")
                yield CopyableLabel(prepare_cmd, prepare_cmd)"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/tui/screens/plan_detail_screen.py","old_string":"            # Prepare commands
            prepare_cmd = f"erk br co --for-plan {self._row.pr_number}"
            with Container(classes="command-row"):
                yield Label("[1]", classes="command-key")
                yield CopyableLabel(prepare_cmd, prepare_cmd)","new_string":"            # Prepare commands
            prepare_cmd = f"erk br co --for-pr {self._row.pr_number}"
            with Container(classes="command-row"):
                yield Label("[1]", classes="command-key")
                yield CopyableLabel(prepare_cmd, prepare_cmd)"}

[Tool: Read] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py"}
[Tool: Read] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"@click.option(
    "--for-plan",
    "for_plan",
    type=str,
    default=None,
    help="PR number or URL with erk-pr label",
)","new_string":"@click.option(
    "--for-pr",
    "for_pr",
    type=str,
    default=None,
    help="PR number or URL with erk-pr label",
)"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"def branch_checkout(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    branch: str | None,
    for_plan: str | None,
    script: bool,
) -> None:","new_string":"def branch_checkout(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    branch: str | None,
    for_pr: str | None,
    script: bool,
) -> None:"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"    """Checkout BRANCH by finding and switching to its worktree.

    Prints the activation path for the target worktree.

    This command finds which worktree has the specified branch checked out
    and switches to it. If the branch exists but isn't checked out anywhere,
    a worktree is automatically created. If the branch exists on origin but
    not locally, a tracking branch and worktree are created automatically.

    Use --for-plan to resolve a PR and set up .erk/impl-context/ after checkout.

    Examples:

        erk br co feature/user-auth      # Checkout existing worktree

        erk br co unchecked-branch       # Auto-create worktree

        erk br co --for-plan 123         # Checkout PR branch with .erk/impl-context/ setup

    If multiple worktrees contain the branch, all options are shown.
    """","new_string":"    """Checkout BRANCH by finding and switching to its worktree.

    Prints the activation path for the target worktree.

    This command finds which worktree has the specified branch checked out
    and switches to it. If the branch exists but isn't checked out anywhere,
    a worktree is automatically created. If the branch exists on origin but
    not locally, a tracking branch and worktree are created automatically.

    Use --for-pr to resolve a PR and set up .erk/impl-context/ after checkout.

    Examples:

        erk br co feature/user-auth      # Checkout existing worktree

        erk br co unchecked-branch       # Auto-create worktree

        erk br co --for-pr 123           # Checkout PR branch with .erk/impl-context/ setup

    If multiple worktrees contain the branch, all options are shown.
    """"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"    handler = script_error_handler(ctx) if script else contextlib.nullcontext()
    with handler:
        _branch_checkout_impl(
            ctx,
            branch=branch,
            for_plan=for_plan,
            script=script,
        )","new_string":"    handler = script_error_handler(ctx) if script else contextlib.nullcontext()
    with handler:
        _branch_checkout_impl(
            ctx,
            branch=branch,
            for_pr=for_pr,
            script=script,
        )"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"def _branch_checkout_impl(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    branch: str | None,
    for_plan: str | None,
    script: bool,
) -> None:","new_string":"def _branch_checkout_impl(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    branch: str | None,
    for_pr: str | None,
    script: bool,
) -> None:"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"    """Implementation body for branch_checkout — separated for error wrapping."""
    # Mutual exclusivity validation
    if for_plan is not None and branch is not None:
        user_output(
            "Error: Cannot specify both BRANCH and --for-plan.\n"
            "Use --for-plan to derive branch name from PR, or provide BRANCH directly."
        )
        raise SystemExit(1) from None

    if for_plan is None and branch is None:
        user_output("Error: Must provide BRANCH argument or --for-plan option.")
        raise SystemExit(1) from None","new_string":"    """Implementation body for branch_checkout — separated for error wrapping."""
    # Mutual exclusivity validation
    if for_pr is not None and branch is not None:
        user_output(
            "Error: Cannot specify both BRANCH and --for-pr.\n"
            "Use --for-pr to derive branch name from PR, or provide BRANCH directly."
        )
        raise SystemExit(1) from None

    if for_pr is None and branch is None:
        user_output("Error: Must provide BRANCH argument or --for-pr option.")
        raise SystemExit(1) from None"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"    # Plan setup - fetches plan and derives branch name if --for-plan is used
    setup: PlanBranchSetup | None = None
    plan: Plan | None = None

    if for_plan is not None:
        pr_number = parse_issue_identifier(for_plan)","new_string":"    # Plan setup - fetches plan and derives branch name if --for-pr is used
    setup: PlanBranchSetup | None = None
    plan: Plan | None = None

    if for_pr is not None:
        pr_number = parse_issue_identifier(for_pr)"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"    # At this point, branch is guaranteed to be set
    assert branch is not None

    # If --for-plan, handle branch creation/tracking before checkout
    if setup is not None:","new_string":"    # At this point, branch is guaranteed to be set
    assert branch is not None

    # If --for-pr, handle branch creation/tracking before checkout
    if setup is not None:"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"            # Root not available or not trunk - checkout in current worktree
            # Ensure branch exists (may need to create tracking branch)
            # Skip if --for-plan already handled branch creation
            if setup is None:","new_string":"            # Root not available or not trunk - checkout in current worktree
            # Ensure branch exists (may need to create tracking branch)
            # Skip if --for-pr already handled branch creation
            if setup is None:"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"    if len(matching_worktrees) == 1:
        # Exactly one worktree contains this branch
        target_worktree = matching_worktrees[0]

        # Set up impl folder if --for-plan was used
        if setup is not None:","new_string":"    if len(matching_worktrees) == 1:
        # Exactly one worktree contains this branch
        target_worktree = matching_worktrees[0]

        # Set up impl folder if --for-pr was used
        if setup is not None:"}

    prints a confirmation message.","new_string":"    """Create impl folder and save plan ref after checkout for --for-pr.

    prints a confirmation message."}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"    """Create impl folder and save plan ref after checkout for --for-plan.

    prints a confirmation message.","new_string":"    """Create impl folder and save plan ref after checkout for --for-pr.

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py","old_string":"        activation_script = render_activation_script(
            worktree_path=worktree_path,
            target_subpath=None,
            post_cd_commands=None,
            final_message=f'echo "Prepared PR #{setup.pr_number} at $(pwd)"',
            comment="erk branch checkout --for-plan activation script",
        )
        result = ctx.script_writer.write_activation_script(
            activation_script,
            command_name="branch-checkout",
            comment=f"branch checkout --for-plan {setup.pr_number}",
        )","new_string":"        activation_script = render_activation_script(
